_description: Green building certification requirements — real credit structures with trackable metrics

leed.name: LEED v4.1
leed.fullName: Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design

greenInfraCredits.name: Construction Activity Pollution Prevention
greenInfraCredits.description: Erosion and sedimentation control plan for all construction activities

greenInfraCredits.name: Site Development — Protect or Restore Habitat
greenInfraCredits.description: Conserve existing natural areas and restore damaged areas with native/adapted vegetation

thresholds.option1: Protect/restore ≥ 40% of site (excl. building footprint) with native vegetation
thresholds.option2: Provide $0.40/sqft for off-site habitat protection via accredited land trust

greenInfraCredits.name: Rainwater Management
greenInfraCredits.description: Manage on-site rainwater to reduce runoff volume and improve water quality

greenInfraCredits.name: Heat Island Reduction
greenInfraCredits.description: Minimize effects on microclimates and human/wildlife habitats through canopy/materials

thresholds.nonroof: Tree canopy within 10yr: ≥ 50% hardscape; or SRI ≥ 29 paving; or combination
thresholds.roof: Vegetated roof ≥ 75%; or SRI-compliant ≥ 75%; or combination

greenInfraCredits.name: Light Pollution Reduction
greenInfraCredits.description: Minimize light trespass, reduce sky-glow, and improve nighttime visibility

greenInfraCredits.name: Optimize Energy Performance
greenInfraCredits.description: Reduce energy cost through efficiency measures including passive design

breeam.name: BREEAM
breeam.fullName: Building Research Establishment Environmental Assessment Methodology

greenInfraCredits.name: Site Selection
greenInfraCredits.description: Encourage use of previously developed and/or contaminated land

greenInfraCredits.name: Ecological Value of Site and Protection
greenInfraCredits.description: Assess and protect existing ecological value of the site

greenInfraCredits.name: Minimising Impact on Existing Site Ecology
greenInfraCredits.description: Minimize negative impact on existing site ecology

greenInfraCredits.name: Enhancement of Ecological Value
greenInfraCredits.description: Enhance the ecological value of the site — 10% Biodiversity Net Gain required (2024)

thresholds.minimum: 10% Biodiversity Net Gain (BNG) — UK law since Feb 2024
thresholds.3credits: Significant net gain with long-term management plan

greenInfraCredits.name: Long-term Biodiversity Management
greenInfraCredits.description: Long-term management and maintenance of site ecology

greenInfraCredits.name: Visual Lighting Design
greenInfraCredits.description: Provide views to nature and daylight access

thresholds.requirement: ≥ 75% of regularly occupied area has views to nature/outdoors

greenInfraCredits.name: Biophilia I — Qualitative
greenInfraCredits.description: Develop a biophilia plan incorporating environmental elements at each design stage

greenInfraCredits.name: Biophilia II — Quantitative
greenInfraCredits.description: Prescribe visual and physical connection with outdoor nature

thresholds.plantWall: Plant wall per floor ≥ 2% of floor area (or largest available wall)
thresholds.pottedPlants: Potted plants or planted beds ≥ 1% of floor area per floor
thresholds.outdoorSpace: Outdoor space with nature accessible within 200m of building entrance

greenInfraCredits.name: Enhanced Air Quality
greenInfraCredits.description: Improve outdoor air quality through green infrastructure

greenGlobes.name: Green Globes
greenGlobes.fullName: Green Globes for New Construction / Existing Buildings

greenInfraCredits.name: Site Selection
greenInfraCredits.description: Encourage selection of previously developed sites, brownfield redevelopment

greenInfraCredits.name: Site Development & Ecological Enhancement
greenInfraCredits.description: Protect and restore natural features, minimize site disturbance

thresholds.erosionControl: Erosion and sediment control during construction
thresholds.nativePlanting: ≥ 50% of landscaped area with native/adapted species
thresholds.canopy: Tree canopy target for site based on climate zone

greenInfraCredits.name: Stormwater Management
greenInfraCredits.description: Manage stormwater through green infrastructure and LID techniques

greenInfraCredits.name: Energy Performance
greenInfraCredits.description: Reduce energy consumption including passive cooling from vegetation
